title: Add monitoring and observability with metrics, structured logging, and alerting
markdown:
## Goal

Add production-grade monitoring and observability to B3nd services: structured logging with log levels, Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint, and health check enhancements for alerting integration. Today, the HTTP server (`libs/b3nd-servers/http.ts`) has a `/api/v1/health` endpoint and the wallet server has basic `console.log` request logging. Production deployments need structured JSON logs, request/response metrics (latency histograms, error rates, request counts), and health checks that expose enough detail for monitoring systems to trigger alerts.

## Background

**Current health endpoints**:
- `libs/b3nd-servers/http.ts` has `GET /api/v1/health` that returns `HealthStatus` (status, message, details)
- `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/core.ts` has `GET /api/v1/health` that returns `{ success, status, server, timestamp }`
- Both return basic JSON but no metrics

**Current logging**:
- The wallet server (`core.ts`) has a request logging middleware that logs `[timestamp] METHOD /path STATUS - Xms` via the `Logger` interface
- The `Logger` interface in `interfaces.ts` has `log()`, `warn()`, `error()` methods
- No structured format, no log levels, no correlation IDs

**What production needs**:
- **Structured logging**: JSON format with timestamp, level, message, request_id, duration_ms, status_code, path, method
- **Metrics**: Request count by method/path/status, latency histogram, active connections, error rate
- **Prometheus endpoint**: `GET /metrics` returning Prometheus text format for scraping
- **Enhanced health**: Include backend connectivity, uptime, version, memory usage

**Constraint**: The B3nd SDK targets Deno, Node.js, and browsers. Metrics collection should use a platform-agnostic approach (in-memory counters, no native dependencies). The Prometheus text format is simple enough to generate without a library.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Structured Logging
- [ ] `StructuredLogger` implementation added to `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/interfaces.ts` that outputs JSON logs
- [ ] Log entries include: timestamp, level, message, request_id (UUID per request), duration_ms, status_code, method, path
- [ ] Log levels supported: debug, info, warn, error
- [ ] Request logging middleware in wallet server updated to use structured format
- [ ] Log level configurable via `WalletServerConfig`

### Metrics
- [ ] In-memory metrics collector tracks: request_count (labels: method, path, status), request_duration_seconds (histogram), active_requests (gauge)
- [ ] `GET /metrics` endpoint returns Prometheus text exposition format
- [ ] Metrics reset on service restart (no persistence needed)
- [ ] Metrics middleware automatically instruments all routes in both `httpServer()` and `WalletServerCore`
- [ ] Histogram buckets: 0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2.5, 5, 10 seconds

### Enhanced Health
- [ ] Health endpoint includes: uptime_seconds, version (from deno.json or package.json), memory_usage_bytes (if available)
- [ ] Health endpoint checks backend connectivity (credential client, proxy client for wallet; storage backend for node)
- [ ] Health returns `degraded` if backend is slow (> 5s response) and `unhealthy` if unreachable
- [ ] Health response includes `checks` array with individual check results

## Implementation Guide

1. Read `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/interfaces.ts` to understand the `Logger` interface and `defaultLogger` implementation.

2. Add `StructuredLogger` to `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/interfaces.ts`:
   ```typescript
   export interface LogEntry {
     timestamp: string;
     level: "debug" | "info" | "warn" | "error";
     message: string;
     request_id?: string;
     duration_ms?: number;
     status_code?: number;
     method?: string;
     path?: string;
     [key: string]: unknown;
   }

   export class StructuredLogger implements Logger {
     constructor(private minLevel: string = "info") {}
     log(message: string, ...args: unknown[]) { this.emit("info", message, args); }
     warn(message: string, ...args: unknown[]) { this.emit("warn", message, args); }
     error(message: string, ...args: unknown[]) { this.emit("error", message, args); }
     debug(message: string, ...args: unknown[]) { this.emit("debug", message, args); }
     private emit(level: string, message: string, args: unknown[]) {
       const entry: LogEntry = { timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), level: level as LogEntry["level"], message };
       console.log(JSON.stringify(entry));
     }
   }
   ```

3. Implement metrics collector (new file or section in `libs/b3nd-servers/metrics.ts`):
   ```typescript
   export class MetricsCollector {
     private counters = new Map<string, number>();
     private histograms = new Map<string, number[]>();
     private gauges = new Map<string, number>();

     incrementCounter(name: string, labels: Record<string, string>): void { ... }
     observeHistogram(name: string, value: number, labels: Record<string, string>): void { ... }
     setGauge(name: string, value: number): void { ... }
     toPrometheusText(): string { ... }
   }
   ```

4. Add metrics middleware to `libs/b3nd-servers/http.ts`:
   - Create a `MetricsCollector` instance per server
   - Wrap each request: increment counter, record start time, observe duration on response
   - Add `GET /metrics` route that calls `collector.toPrometheusText()`
   - Track `b3nd_http_requests_total{method, path, status}`, `b3nd_http_request_duration_seconds{method, path}`, `b3nd_http_active_requests`

5. Add the same metrics middleware to `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/core.ts`:
   - Insert after CORS, before routes (alongside the request logging middleware)
   - Add `GET /metrics` route
   - Track wallet-specific metrics: `b3nd_wallet_auth_total{action, status}`, `b3nd_wallet_proxy_total{operation, status}`

6. Enhance health endpoints:
   - In `libs/b3nd-servers/http.ts`: record server start time, include `uptime_seconds`, attempt `backend.read.health()` with timeout
   - In `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/core.ts`: ping credential and proxy clients, include response times, determine overall status

7. Update `WalletServerConfig` in `types.ts` with optional `logLevel: "debug" | "info" | "warn" | "error"`.

## Files to Create/Modify

- `libs/b3nd-servers/metrics.ts` -- New file: `MetricsCollector` class with Prometheus text output
- `libs/b3nd-servers/http.ts` -- Add metrics middleware, `/metrics` endpoint, enhanced health
- `libs/b3nd-servers/mod.ts` -- Export `MetricsCollector`
- `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/interfaces.ts` -- Add `StructuredLogger`, `LogEntry`
- `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/core.ts` -- Add metrics middleware, `/metrics` endpoint, enhanced health, structured logging
- `libs/b3nd-wallet-server/types.ts` -- Add `logLevel` to config

## Testing

```bash
deno test libs/b3nd-servers/
deno test libs/b3nd-wallet-server/ --ignore=libs/b3nd-client-localstorage
```

Specific test scenarios:
- `MetricsCollector.toPrometheusText()` produces valid Prometheus text format
- Counter increments correctly with labels
- Histogram records values in correct buckets
- `/metrics` endpoint returns text/plain content type
- Health endpoint returns `degraded` when backend is slow (mock slow response)
- Health endpoint returns `unhealthy` when backend is unreachable
- `StructuredLogger` outputs valid JSON
- Request logging middleware includes request_id, duration_ms, status_code

## Dependencies

No hard prerequisites. This is foundational infrastructure that benefits all services. The metrics and logging patterns established here will be used by all subsequent stories.
tasksExcerpt:
- [ ] Add monitoring/observability (health endpoints exist, need metrics, logging, alerting)
- [ ] Harden Docker images for production
- [ ] Document production deployment guide
- [ ] Implement rate limiting at HTTP layer (DoS prevention, separate from protocol-level gas)

---

## F. Content & Marketing
